message.content:
{
  "competency_groups": [
    {
      "code": "C12-PHYS-U1-C1",
      "title": "Electric Charges and Fields",
      "description": "Understanding electric charges, conservation of charge, Coulomb's law, superposition principle, continuous charge distribution, electric field, electric field lines, electric dipole, torque on a dipole, electric flux, and Gauss's theorem applications.",
      "competencies": [
        "Apply Coulomb's law to calculate force between point charges",
        "Use superposition principle for forces between multiple charges",
        "Analyze continuous charge distribution scenarios",
        "Describe electric field due to point charge and dipole",
        "Interpret electric field lines and flux",
        "Apply Gauss's theorem to find fields of symmetric charge distributions"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit 1",
      "chapter_name": "Chapter–1: Electric Charges and Fields"
    },
    {
      "code": "C12-PHYS-U1-C2",
      "title": "Electrostatic Potential and Capacitance",
      "description": "Mastering concepts of electric potential, potential difference, equipotential surfaces, electrical potential energy, conductors, dielectrics, capacitors, capacitance, and energy storage.",
      "competencies": [
        "Calculate electric potential and potential difference",
        "Determine electrical potential energy of charge systems",
        "Analyze behavior of conductors and dielectrics",
        "Compute capacitance for various configurations",
        "Evaluate energy stored in capacitors"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit 1",
      "chapter_name": "Chapter–2: Electrostatic Potential and Capacitance"
    },
    {
      "code": "C12-PHYS-U2-C3",
      "title": "Current Electricity",
      "description": "Exploring electric current, drift velocity, Ohm's law, resistivity, internal resistance, Kirchhoff's rules, and Wheatstone bridge.",
      "competencies": [
        "Relate drift velocity to electric current",
        "Apply Ohm's law to linear and non-linear circuits",
        "Calculate resistivity and conductivity",
        "Analyze temperature dependence of resistance",
        "Use Kirchhoff's rules for circuit analysis",
        "Operate Wheatstone bridge for resistance measurement"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit 2",
      "chapter_name": "Chapter–3: Current Electricity"
    },
    {
      "code": "C12-PHYS-U3-C4",
      "title": "Moving Charges and Magnetism",
      "description": "Investigating magnetic fields, Biot-Savart law, Ampere's law, forces on moving charges and current-carrying conductors, torque on current loops, and galvanometers.",
      "competencies": [
        "Apply Biot-Savart law to calculate magnetic fields",
        "Use Ampere's law for symmetric current distributions",
        "Calculate force on moving charges in magnetic fields",
        "Determine force between parallel current-carrying conductors",
        "Analyze torque on current loops and magnetic dipoles"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit 3",
      "chapter_name": "Chapter–4: Moving Charges and Magnetism"
    },
    {
      "code": "C12-PHYS-U3-C5",
      "title": "Magnetism and Matter",
      "description": "Understanding magnetic properties of materials, magnetic dipoles, magnetization, and temperature effects on magnetic properties.",
      "competencies": [
        "Classify materials as para-, dia-, or ferro-magnetic",
        "Analyze magnetic field intensity due to dipoles",
        "Evaluate torque on magnetic dipoles in uniform fields",
        "Explain magnetization and temperature effects"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit 3",
      "chapter_name": "Chapter–5: Magnetism and Matter"
    },
    {
      "code": "C12-PHYS-U4-C6",
      "title": "Electromagnetic Induction",
      "description": "Studying electromagnetic induction, Faraday's laws, Lenz's law, self and mutual induction.",
      "competencies": [
        "Apply Faraday's laws to calculate induced EMF",
        "Use Lenz's law to determine direction of induced current",
        "Analyze self and mutual induction phenomena"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit 4",
      "chapter_name": "Chapter–6: Electromagnetic Induction"
    },
    {
      "code": "C12-PHYS-U4-C7",
      "title": "Alternating Current",
      "description": "Exploring AC characteristics, reactance, impedance, LCR circuits, resonance, power factor, and transformers.",
      "competencies": [
        "Calculate peak and RMS values of AC quantities",
        "Analyze reactance and impedance in AC circuits",
        "Study resonance in LCR series circuits",
        "Evaluate power factor and wattless current"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit 4",
      "chapter_name": "Chapter–7: Alternating Current"
    },
    {
      "code": "C12-PHYS-U5-C8",
      "title": "Electromagnetic Waves",
      "description": "Understanding displacement current, electromagnetic waves characteristics, transverse nature, and the electromagnetic spectrum.",
      "competencies": [
        "Explain the concept of displacement current",
        "Describe characteristics and transverse nature of EM waves",
        "Identify regions of the electromagnetic spectrum"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit 5",
      "chapter_name": "Chapter–8: Electromagnetic Waves"
    },
    {
      "code": "C12-PHYS-U6-C9",
      "title": "Ray Optics and Optical Instruments",
      "description": "Mastering reflection, refraction, spherical mirrors, lenses, optical instruments, and their magnifying powers.",
      "competencies": [
        "Apply mirror and lens formulas to image formation",
        "Analyze total internal reflection and optical fibers",
        "Calculate magnification and power of optical instruments"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit 6",
      "chapter_name": "Chapter–9: Ray Optics and Optical Instruments"
    },
    {
      "code": "C12-PHYS-U6-C10",
      "title": "Wave Optics",
      "description": "Studying wave fronts, Huygen's principle, interference, diffraction, and coherence.",
      "competencies": [
        "Apply Huygen's principle to reflection and refraction",
        "Analyze interference patterns and fringe width",
        "Explain diffraction phenomena qualitatively"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit 6",
      "chapter_name": "Chapter–10: Wave Optics"
    },
    {
      "code": "C12-PHYS-U7-C11",
      "title": "Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter",
      "description": "Exploring photoelectric effect, particle nature of light, matter waves, and de-Broglie relation.",
      "competencies": [
        "Explain photoelectric effect using Einstein's equation",
        "Apply de-Broglie relation to matter waves"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit 7",
      "chapter_name": "Chapter–11: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter"
    },
    {
      "code": "C12-PHYS-U8-C12",
      "title": "Atoms",
      "description": "Understanding atomic models, Bohr model, hydrogen spectra, and electron orbits.",
      "competencies": [
        "Describe Rutherford's and Bohr's atomic models",
        "Calculate radius, velocity, and energy of electron orbits"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit 8",
      "chapter_name": "Chapter–12: Atoms"
    },
    {
      "code": "C12-PHYS-U8-C13",
      "title": "Nuclei",
      "description": "Studying nuclear composition, binding energy, mass-energy relation, fission, and fusion.",
      "competencies": [
        "Explain nuclear force and binding energy per nucleon",
        "Analyze nuclear fission and fusion processes"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit 8",
      "chapter_name": "Chapter–13: Nuclei"
    },
    {
      "code": "C12-PHYS-U9-C14",
      "title": "Electronic Devices",
      "description": "Understanding energy bands, semiconductors, p-n junctions, diodes, and their applications.",
      "competencies": [
        "Classify materials based on energy bands",
        "Analyze I-V characteristics of p-n junction diodes",
        "Apply diodes as rectifiers in circuits"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit 9",
      "chapter_name": "Chapter–14: Semiconductor Electronics: Materials, Devices and Simple Circuits"
    }
  ]
}
